# Office Oxide — The Fastest Office Document Library for Python, Rust & WASM

> The fastest library for Office document processing.
> Text extraction, markdown, HTML, creation, and editing for DOCX, XLSX, PPTX, DOC, XLS, PPT.
> Up to 100x faster than python-docx, openpyxl, python-pptx.
> 98.1% pass rate on 6,062 real-world files. Zero failures on valid documents.
> MIT / Apache-2.0 license. Current version: 0.1.0.

- Python: `pip install office-oxide`
- Rust: `cargo add office_oxide`
- JavaScript/WASM: `npm install office-oxide-wasm`
- CLI: `cargo install office_oxide_cli`
- MCP Server: `cargo install office_oxide_mcp`
- Documentation: https://office.oxide.fyi
- GitHub: https://github.com/yfedoseev/office_oxide

## Supported Formats

6 formats: DOCX, XLSX, PPTX (modern OOXML) and DOC, XLS, PPT (legacy binary).
Output: plain text, markdown, HTML, JSON IR, CSV (XLSX only).
Read, write, and edit OOXML formats. Read + convert legacy formats.

## Getting Started

- [README](https://raw.githubusercontent.com/yfedoseev/office_oxide/main/README.md): Full overview, benchmarks, installation, API examples
- [Rust API (docs.rs)](https://docs.rs/office_oxide): Complete Rust API reference
- [Architecture](https://raw.githubusercontent.com/yfedoseev/office_oxide/main/docs/ARCHITECTURE.md): Module structure and design decisions
- [Changelog](https://raw.githubusercontent.com/yfedoseev/office_oxide/main/CHANGELOG.md): Version history

## Python API

```python
from office_oxide import Document, extract_text, to_markdown, to_html

text = extract_text("report.docx")
markdown = to_markdown("data.xlsx")
html = to_html("slides.pptx")

doc = Document.open("file.docx")
doc.plain_text()
doc.to_markdown()
doc.to_html()
doc.to_ir()       # Structured dict
doc.save_as("output.docx")
```

## Rust API

```rust
use office_oxide::Document;
let doc = Document::open("report.docx")?;
doc.plain_text();
doc.to_markdown();
doc.to_html();
doc.to_ir();  // DocumentIR struct
```

Sub-modules: office_oxide::{docx, xlsx, pptx, doc, xls, ppt, create, edit, ir}

## JavaScript/WASM API

```javascript
const { WasmDocument } = require("office-oxide-wasm");
const doc = new WasmDocument(buffer, "docx");
doc.plainText();
doc.toMarkdown();
doc.toHtml();
doc.toIr();
```

## CLI

```bash
office-oxide text report.docx
office-oxide markdown data.xlsx
office-oxide html slides.pptx
office-oxide ir document.docx
office-oxide info file.pptx
```

## MCP Server

Gives Claude, Cursor, and AI assistants the ability to read Office documents locally.
Tools: extract (text/markdown/html/ir), info.
Config: `{"mcpServers": {"office-oxide": {"command": "office-oxide-mcp"}}}`

## Performance

Benchmarked on 6,062 files from 11 open-source test suites.
DOCX: 7ms mean (8-100x faster than python-docx, mammoth, markitdown).
XLSX: 6ms mean (matches calamine, 40x faster than openpyxl).
PPTX: 3ms mean (8x faster than python-pptx).
Legacy DOC: 2ms, XLS: 9ms, PPT: 6ms.
98.1% overall pass rate. All failures are genuinely invalid files (CVE exploits, corrupted, encrypted).

## Use Cases

- RAG / LLM pipelines: Extract clean text or markdown for retrieval-augmented generation
- Document processing at scale: Parse thousands of documents in seconds
- AI assistants: MCP server for Claude, Cursor, and other tools
- Format conversion: Convert between formats via intermediate representation
- Drop-in replacement for python-docx, openpyxl, python-pptx, xlrd
